c1.speaker: narrator
c1.line: Ida Wren has the Ceremonial Key's lock on a velvet cloth, oiling it with a brush the size of an eyelash.

c2.speaker: ida
c2.line: There now. One last time before your big night. Fifty years and never a squeak — because somebody ASKS it how it's doing, don't they. Yes they do.

c3.speaker: gary
c3.line: You made that lock, didn't you? At the founding?

c4.speaker: ida
c4.line: Apprentice hands, Mr. Gibbons, and my best work for forty years after. It'll turn tonight like it turned the first day. Locks keep their promises. It's the rest of us that wobble.
